preamble: Intervocalic voicing of the voiceless dental fricative [θ] to its voiced counterpart [ð] is a regular phonological process in many Germanic and other language varieties, typically occurring when the sound is positioned between two voiced segments.

examples.note: Voicing of the final dental fricative when followed by a voiced suffix.
Intervocalic voicing in historical verb formation.
Dental fricatives became voiced when positioned between vowels (e.g., 'fathom').
